sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.3g of fat. cholesterol adds to the tradeoff. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.

sections.narration: 0.2g of carbs. everything else is fairly underwhelming. in this category, carbs barely matter.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 24g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin b12 at 54% daily value stands out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first: high satiety protein source because that is a major real-world strength for meat. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. easy to centre a meal around because practical meal value matters.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: cholesterol headline is hard to ignore because even if the full story is more nuanced, the category score should reflect how extreme the number looks. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count. brings no fibre support because that leaves a nutrition gap the rest of the meal has to cover.

closing.summary: Shrimp does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
